corsairCoveReleaseDate.title: Corsair Cove Release Date, Price, and PC Requirements
corsairCoveReleaseDate.intro: Corsair Cove is a single-player pirate city builder developed by Limbic Entertainment and published by Hooded Horse. The game launches for Windows PC on July 31, 2026 across Steam, the Epic Games Store, the Microsoft Store, and PC Game Pass.

items.details: Corsair Cove is listed as Coming Soon ahead of its PC launch.
Corsair Cove launches on PC with no console version listed on the official store pages.

items.value: Steam, Epic Games Store, Microsoft Store
items.details: Corsair Cove can be wishlisted before launch on every supported storefront.
PC Game Pass access to Corsair Cove begins when the game launches on July 31, 2026.
Standard digital PC edition of Corsair Cove.

Corsair Cove supports English, French, Italian, German, Spanish, Korean, Polish, Russian, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, Japanese, and Portuguese-Brazil, with full audio in English, French, and German.

items.value: Windows 10 64-bit, 8 GB RAM, DirectX 12, 30 GB storage
items.details: Intel Core i7-7700 or AMD Ryzen 5 1600; NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 Super 6 GB or AMD Radeon RX 5600 XT 6 GB. SSD recommended for Corsair Cove.

items.value: Windows 10 64-bit, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 12, 30 GB storage
items.details: Intel Core i5-9600K or AMD Ryzen 5 3600; NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 8 GB or AMD Radeon RX 6600 XT 8 GB. SSD recommended for Corsair Cove.

corsairCoveBeginnerGuide.title: Corsair Cove Demo Beginner Guide
corsairCoveBeginnerGuide.intro: Download the free Corsair Cove demo, establish a connected settlement, assign pirates correctly, and build a stable first production network before expanding.

steps.title: Install the Free Demo
steps.description: Open the Corsair Cove Demo page on Steam and select Install Demo. The demo is single-player, supports Steam Cloud, and uses the same minimum PC requirements as the full game.
steps.tip: Install the Corsair Cove demo on an SSD when possible.

steps.title: Read the Island View
steps.description: Use the Island View as your main management screen in Corsair Cove. Track Main Quests, Side Quests, Deeds, settlement notifications, available Coin, Pirate Camp satisfaction, Cohesion, and the current game speed.
steps.tip: Pause Corsair Cove while reading a new quest or planning a large construction step.

steps.title: Create the First Connected Settlement
steps.description: Use the Construction tab to place buildings and connect them to the settlement. Automatic connections appear when placement allows them; manual connections are required when a building does not link automatically.
steps.tip: A building cannot operate unless it is connected. Green connection arrows mark useful hubs in Corsair Cove.

steps.title: Manage Drifters and Assigned Pirates
steps.description: Drifters are unemployed pirates with no upkeep who construct buildings and still need housing. Assigned Pirates work specific jobs, require housing, and add upkeep costs.
steps.tip: Do not assign every pirate immediately. Keep enough Drifters free to finish construction in Corsair Cove.

steps.title: Stabilize Housing, Food, and Drink
steps.description: Provide housing and watch the needs of pirates living inside each camp. Use the Resources and Pirate Camp overviews to compare production, consumption, and camp satisfaction in real time.
steps.tip: Growing population too quickly can make Corsair Cove Cohesion and basic supplies harder to maintain.

steps.title: Build Short Production Routes
steps.description: Connect resource nodes to production buildings and finished goods to distribution hubs. Select a resource building to inspect its logistics lines: green is short and healthy, yellow is suboptimal, and red is long enough to create production problems.
steps.tip: Move buildings or add a better connection before a red route becomes a settlement-wide bottleneck in Corsair Cove.

steps.title: Expand with a New Pirate Camp
steps.description: Place another Pirate Camp when distant resources fall outside the current operating area. Each camp expands the buildable radius but must support the housing, food, drink, and logistics needs of its own residents.
steps.tip: Do not create a remote camp in Corsair Cove until a reliable supply route can reach it.

steps.title: Unlock Progress and Prepare a Ship
steps.description: Complete Deeds and quests to earn Principle Points for Notoriety, Empire, Seafaring, and Wealth. Build a ship at the Pier, assign a captain, compare ship stats with event requirements, and carry enough Supplies before sailing.
steps.tip: Pause low-priority buildings to free workers in Corsair Cove, and check that a stalled building is not accidentally paused.

quickTips: Every working building in Corsair Cove must stay connected to the settlement.
Keep a Drifter reserve available for construction instead of assigning every pirate at once.
Watch the Resources overview to catch negative production-versus-consumption before stockpiles empty.
Pause the game while planning large construction steps or reading a new quest.

corsairCoveBestLayout.title: Best Corsair Cove Layout Patterns for Cliffs and Vertical Building
corsairCoveBestLayout.intro: Use compact hubs, layered production terraces, bridges, ziplines, and carefully supplied Pirate Camps to reduce travel across the steep terrain of Corsair Cove.

cards.name: Connected Harbor Core
cards.pattern: Create one short connection spine around the starting harbor, then place early housing, the first Pirate Camp, core services, and distribution access beside it.
cards.benefit: Keeps the first workforce and essential goods on short routes while leaving cliff edges available for later expansion in Corsair Cove.

cards.name: Cliffside Production Terrace
cards.pattern: Place resource extraction near its node, processing buildings on the nearest usable ledge, and a distribution hub on the level that connects most easily to housing and the harbor.
cards.benefit: Turns vertical terrain into a layered production district without sending every worker and item across the full island.

cards.name: Bridge and Hub Crossroads
cards.bestFor: Crossing chasms and joining several districts
cards.pattern: Use bridges to connect opposite ledges and place connection hubs where multiple routes meet. Route several nearby buildings through the same well-positioned junction.
cards.benefit: Reduces duplicate paths and gives the Corsair Cove settlement a clear central route between production, housing, and distribution.

cards.name: Zipline Vertical Express
cards.bestFor: Fast movement between high and low districts
cards.pattern: Unlock ziplines through Pirate Principles and place them on routes with a large height difference, especially between upper resource areas and lower production or harbor districts.
cards.benefit: Shortens steep vertical travel that would otherwise require long winding connections in Corsair Cove.

cards.name: Remote Resource Camp
cards.bestFor: Iron, copper, lumber, fiber, and distant deposits
cards.pattern: Build a Pirate Camp near a valuable resource cluster, keep extraction and first-stage production inside its operating radius, and connect the outpost through one dependable route.
cards.benefit: Expands construction range while preventing workers from crossing the entire island for every task.

cards.name: Dock Import Loop
cards.pattern: Connect the dock directly to nearby distribution and the production buildings that consume imported or looted resources.
cards.benefit: The dock acts as a resource source, letting grain, coin, whale blubber, silver, and wine enter Corsair Cove with minimal extra travel.

corsairCoveProductionChains.title: Corsair Cove Production Chains, Goods, and Bottleneck Fixes
corsairCoveProductionChains.intro: Manage more than 50 goods across local resource nodes, production buildings, distribution hubs, Pirate Camps, ships, and off-island raids in Corsair Cove.

chains.label: Local Resource Network
flow: Resource nodes (iron, copper, lumber, fiber, stone)
chains.management: Place extraction beside the node, keep the first processing step nearby, and avoid sending raw materials through red-distance routes.

chains.label: Stone Branch
flow: Stone and Stone Blocks or Charcoal
chains.management: Stone feeds more than one production branch in Corsair Cove, so compare both outputs against consumption before assigning all supply to one destination.

chains.label: Food and Drink Supply
chains.management: Keep basic provisions close to populated camps. Every new Pirate Camp adds another area whose residents need reliable food and drink.

chains.label: Clothing and Luxury Goods
chains.management: Advanced pirates demand better goods than basic crews. Expand these chains only after food, drink, housing, and Cohesion are stable in Corsair Cove.

chains.label: Ship and Combat Equipment
chains.management: Powerful ships and specialist crews require advanced equipment. Keep military production connected to the Pier and maintain ammunition for defensive fortifications.

chains.label: Off-Island Supply
flow: Looted or imported resources (grain, coin, silver, wine)
chains.management: These resources are obtained by sailing beyond the home island. Connect the dock into the relevant chain and treat expedition planning as part of the Corsair Cove economy.

chains.label: Bottleneck Diagnostic Loop
flow: Inspect green, yellow, and red route lines
Shorten the route or improve the connection
Pause low-priority buildings to free workers
Recheck supply at every Pirate Camp
chains.management: Green routes are healthy and short, yellow routes are suboptimal, and red routes are long enough to cause production issues in Corsair Cove. Confirm stalled buildings are connected and not paused.

corsairCoveShipsAndCombat.title: Corsair Cove Ships, Captains, and Naval Combat
corsairCoveShipsAndCombat.intro: Ships are built at the Pier and sent to events on the World Map of Corsair Cove. A captain adds unique traits and event abilities, so the best fleet is the one matched to the exact encounter.

ships.name: Landlubber
ships.use: A baseline Corsair Cove vessel for early events whose recommended metrics fit its displayed ratings.

ships.name: Steel Edge
ships.use: An improved design with a larger frame, more durability, and stronger ratings for tougher encounters in Corsair Cove.

combatSteps.title: Read the objective
combatSteps.detail: A Corsair Cove event may ask you to reduce enemy Health to zero, close the distance between ships, or collect a required amount of goods.

combatSteps.title: Check the enemy action
combatSteps.detail: The opponent reveals its planned action each turn. Choose whether to counter that action or advance the event objective.

combatSteps.title: Spend Supplies on an action
combatSteps.detail: Most Battle cards cost Supplies. When Supplies are low, switch to Support cards to restore them before returning to Battle actions.

combatSteps.title: Roll the dice pools
combatSteps.detail: Both sides roll their active dice in Corsair Cove combat. A higher result improves the outcome, but attacks aimed at different areas can both resolve in the same turn.

combatSteps.title: Counter the same area
combatSteps.detail: An enemy action is fully negated only when your action counters the same target area and your dice result is higher.

combatSteps.title: Protect Health and Crew
combatSteps.detail: The Corsair Cove encounter is lost if either Ship Health or Crew reaches zero. Combat stats determine how effective each card is.

prepTips: Compare every recommended event metric with the ship's individual ratings, not only the overall level.
Restore Ship Health, Crew, and Supplies before entering a high-risk Corsair Cove event.
Switch to a captain whose traits and event ability directly support the objective.
Keep island fortresses and gun towers supplied with Cannonballs and Black Powder.

corsairCoveCrewAndCohesion.title: Corsair Cove Crew Jobs, Needs, and Cohesion
corsairCoveCrewAndCohesion.intro: Keep workers housed, sailors supplied, and every Pirate Camp satisfied in Corsair Cove. Unmet needs can drive pirates away, while zero Cohesion ends the run.

roles.detail: Unemployed reserve crew required to construct buildings. Keep a reserve available instead of assigning every pirate at once.
Construct and expand the settlement. Pause lower-priority buildings when labor is needed elsewhere.
Move goods through the logistics network. Short green connections are healthier than yellow or red long-distance routes.
Operate resource buildings and production chains at different skill levels. Watch real-time production and consumption before adding another workplace.
Crew early ships and sea events. Bread and booze support early growth, but advanced crew soon demand finer goods.
Bombardiers, Navigators, Marauders, and Renegades for more demanding fleet roles. Build complete equipment chains before recruiting specialists in numbers.
Veteran Corsairs and descendants of the first Buccaneers. Elite demand chains require both island production and world-event goods.

cohesionTips: Build Housing before assigning new workers or expanding specialist crews.
Use the Resources panel to fix negative production-versus-consumption before stockpiles empty.
Supply every Pirate Camp locally with food and drink instead of relying on the main camp alone.
Slow population growth when Corsair Cove Cohesion is low; pirates abandon the settlement when needs are ignored.

corsairCoveCompassQuests.title: Corsair Cove Compass Quests and World Events
corsairCoveCompassQuests.intro: Earn Principle Points through Deeds and Side Quests, shape the four Compass paths, and send purpose-built ships into raids, treasure hunts, forts, fleets, and legendary encounters in Corsair Cove.

principles.name: Notoriety
principles.detail: Spend Principle Points on the Notoriety branch to unlock its missions and branch-specific benefits in Corsair Cove.

principles.name: Empire
principles.detail: Spend Principle Points on the Empire branch to unlock a larger, more capable haven.

principles.name: Seafaring
principles.detail: Spend Principle Points on the Seafaring branch to unlock stronger fleet options and broader event capability.

principles.name: Wealth
principles.detail: Spend Principle Points on the Wealth branch to unlock a resource-rich production economy in Corsair Cove.

events.name: Smuggler Transport
events.objective: Help smugglers move their wares across the Seven Seas.
events.detail: Use a ship and captain whose event ratings meet the icon's recommendations.

events.name: Ancient Temple Expedition
events.objective: Search dangerous temple sites for treasure.
events.detail: Bring enough Crew and Supplies to survive a multi-step expedition in Corsair Cove.

events.name: Raid
events.objective: Pillage nearby islands for goods the settlement needs.
events.detail: Compare all recommended ratings and select a captain suited to the event type.

events.name: Enemy Fort
events.objective: Attack a Crown stronghold or fortified target.
events.detail: Use an upgraded ship with full Health, Crew, and Supplies.

events.name: Enemy Fleet
events.objective: Fight Crown ships in turn-based naval combat.
events.detail: Prepare Health damage, Crew damage, defensive counters, and Supply recovery cards.

events.name: Legendary Creature
events.objective: Confront monstrous whales, Krakens, and otherworldly sea threats.
events.detail: Use stronger ships, suitable captains, and deep reserves for high-risk Corsair Cove objectives.

corsairCoveUpdates.title: Corsair Cove Updates and News
corsairCoveUpdates.intro: Follow release announcements, demo patches, event appearances, and the Legends Before the Storm character series from official Corsair Cove channels.

entries.title: Legends Before the Storm: Rambullion, the Tavernkeep
entries.summary: The former pirate Tadhg MacMhuircheartaigh survives the fall of an old pirate haven by hiding as Rambullion, an observant tavernkeep waiting for a chance to revive the dream.

entries.title: Legends Before the Storm: Teach, the Brute
entries.summary: Edward Teach, better known as Blackbeard, is presented as the last of the old guard: a brutal survivor who joins the prisoners' escape plan.

entries.title: Legends Before the Storm: Kanja, the Gambler
entries.summary: Kanja Shan rises from wealthy spice merchant to pirate sponsor, loses his fortune and fleet, and is captured while planning his next play.

entries.title: Legends Before the Storm: Scarlet, the Fearless
entries.summary: Scarlet Drake pursues fame rather than coin, grows from an Irish orphan into a daring captain, and seeks a way to defeat the cursed Reaper.

entries.title: Legends Before the Storm: Honorata, the Adventurer
entries.summary: Honorata Van Horn rejects her merchant-family future, learns navigation and command, steals a company ship, and hunts the Seven Seas' mythical creatures.

entries.title: Play Corsair Cove Demo During Steam Next Fest 2026
entries.summary: The official announcement invited players to build a pirate haven, manage crafting chains, raise a fleet, and fight the Crown during Steam Next Fest.

entries.title: Demo Update #1: Crash Fixes and Tutorial Fix
entries.summary: The first Corsair Cove demo hotfix addressed random crashes, a quality-settings crash, a tutorial goal that could stall below 10 Crew, and a pause-state issue after a ship returned.

entries.title: Corsair Cove Releases July 31, 2026
entries.summary: Limbic Entertainment and Hooded Horse confirmed the July 31, 2026 launch date and made the free demo available alongside the release-date trailer.

entries.title: Hooded Horse and Limbic Entertainment Set Sail Together
entries.summary: The reveal introduced free-form vertical construction, more than 50 resources, bridges, ziplines, crew needs, shipbuilding, and the four Compass progression paths.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Corsair Cove

questions.question: When does Corsair Cove release?
questions.answer: Corsair Cove launches for Windows PC on July 31, 2026 across Steam, the Epic Games Store, the Microsoft Store, and PC Game Pass.

questions.question: Is there a free Corsair Cove demo?
questions.answer: Yes. The Corsair Cove demo is free on Steam and lets you build a connected settlement, assign pirates, and start a production network before the full launch.

questions.question: Where can I play Corsair Cove?
questions.answer: Corsair Cove is available on Steam, the Epic Games Store, and the Microsoft Store for Windows PC, with PC Game Pass access on day one.

questions.question: How much does Corsair Cove cost?
questions.answer: The standard digital PC edition of Corsair Cove is $29.99 USD.

questions.question: What is the Compass in Corsair Cove?
questions.answer: The Compass is the Corsair Cove progression system. You earn Principle Points from Deeds and Side Quests and spend them across Notoriety, Empire, Seafaring, and Wealth to unlock missions, buildings, chains, and ships.

questions.question: Is Corsair Cove single-player?
questions.answer: Yes. Corsair Cove is a single-player pirate city builder with no multiplayer or co-op.

questions.question: What are the Corsair Cove PC requirements?
questions.answer: Corsair Cove needs a DirectX 12-capable Windows 10 64-bit PC with 8 GB RAM minimum and an NVIDIA GTX 1660 Super or AMD RX 5600 XT, with an SSD recommended.

questions.question: How many goods are in Corsair Cove?
questions.answer: Corsair Cove includes more than 50 goods across food, drink, clothing, equipment, weapons, construction materials, and event resources.
